assertion_results.text: outputs/claude-md-result.md preserves the existing trustify-backend entry in the Repository Registry unchanged (same Role, Serena Instance, Path values as in claude-md-configured.md)
assertion_results.evidence: claude-md-result.md line 16 contains: '| trustify-backend | Rust backend service | serena_backend | /home/user/trustify-backend |' which matches the original values exactly (Role: Rust backend service, Serena Instance: serena_backend, Path: /home/user/trustify-backend).

assertion_results.text: outputs/claude-md-result.md adds a new trustify-ui entry to the Repository Registry with serena_ui as the Serena Instance
assertion_results.evidence: claude-md-result.md line 17 contains: '| trustify-ui | TypeScript frontend | serena_ui | /home/user/trustify-ui |' — a new row with serena_ui as the Serena Instance.

assertion_results.text: outputs/claude-md-result.md preserves all existing Jira Configuration fields unchanged — Project key remains TC, Cloud ID, Feature issue type ID remain as in claude-md-configured.md

assertion_results.text: outputs/changes-log.md explicitly identifies which entries were preserved (existing) and which were added (new)
assertion_results.evidence: changes-log.md has an '## Added' section (lines 8-27) listing the new trustify-ui row and serena_ui limitation entry, and a '## Preserved (no changes)' section (lines 29-68) listing the existing trustify-backend row, Jira Configuration, Code Intelligence body text, serena_backend limitation, Bug Configuration, and non-configuration content.

assertion_results.text: outputs/discovery-log.md notes that serena_backend was already configured and serena_ui is newly discovered
assertion_results.evidence: discovery-log.md lines 27-28: 'serena_backend — already in Repository Registry (trustify-backend). No action needed.' and 'serena_ui — NOT in Repository Registry. New entry required.'

assertion_results.text: The existing Code Intelligence section content (naming convention, example, serena_backend limitations) is preserved — only new serena_ui content is added
assertion_results.evidence: claude-md-result.md lines 29-42 preserve the naming convention ('Tools are prefixed by Serena instance name: `mcp__<instance>__<tool>`.'), the serena_backend example (mcp__serena_backend__find_symbol call), and the serena_backend limitation ('serena_backend: rust-analyzer may take 30-60 seconds to index on first use'). The only addition is line 43: '- `serena_ui`: No known limitations'.

assertion_results.text: outputs/claude-md-result.md preserves the existing '## Bug Configuration' section with Bug issue type ID (10001), Bug template (docs/bug-template.md), and Bug-to-Task link type (Blocks) unchanged — the skill reports Bug Configuration is up to date
assertion_results.evidence: claude-md-result.md lines 45-49 show: '## Bug Configuration' with Bug issue type ID: 10001, Bug template: docs/bug-template.md, Bug-to-Task link type: Blocks — all unchanged. discovery-log.md lines 69-74 report: 'Bug Configuration is up to date. All three required fields are populated' with 'No changes needed.'

assertion_results.text: The skill offers Security Configuration opt-in since the existing CLAUDE.md has no '## Security Configuration' section, and respects the user's decline — no Security Configuration section appears in the output
assertion_results.evidence: discovery-log.md lines 76-78: 'User was asked whether to enable security triage for this project. User declined. Security Configuration was not scaffolded. Skipped.' claude-md-result.md contains no '## Security Configuration' section (file ends at line 50 after Bug Configuration). changes-log.md lines 80-82 confirm: 'Security Configuration — Not created. User declined when asked whether to enable security triage.'
